annotations.label: Dirac delta function

anchor.snippet: the Dirac delta function (or

annotations.note: The Dirac delta is formalized both as `Measure.dirac`, `Distribution.delta`, and `TemperedDistribution.delta`.

annotations.label: Heuristic characterization

anchor.snippet: can be loosely thought of as a function on the real line which is zero everywhere except at the origin

annotations.note: This is an informal heuristic description of δ and has no formalized counterpart.

annotations.label: Dirac measure

anchor.snippet: to define a measure , called Dirac measure

annotations.note: Defined as `MeasureTheory.Measure.dirac` with `dirac_apply_of_mem` giving the value 1 on sets containing the point.

annotations.label: Delta measure is singular

anchor.snippet: The measure [MATH] is not absolutely continuous with respect to the Lebesgue measure

annotations.note: `mutuallySingular_dirac` shows `dirac x ⟂ₘ μ` for any `NoAtoms μ`, which specializes to Lebesgue and implies non-absolute-continuity.

annotations.label: CDF of delta measure is unit step

anchor.snippet: the delta measure is characterized by its cumulative distribution function , which is the unit step function

annotations.note: No declaration computes the CDF of the Dirac measure as the Heaviside step function.

annotations.label: Moments of delta measure

anchor.snippet: All higher moments of [MATH] are zero

annotations.note: No lemma identifies the higher moments of the Dirac measure as zero.

annotations.label: Delta distribution

anchor.snippet: the Dirac delta is a linear functional on the space of test functions

annotations.note: `Distribution.delta` is the linear functional `f ↦ f x` on test functions in `𝓓^{n}(Ω, ℝ)`.

annotations.label: Continuity criterion for distributions

anchor.snippet: it is necessary and sufficient that, for every positive integer

annotations.note: Mathlib characterizes continuity on 𝓓 via the LF inductive-limit structure, but the explicit seminorm/C·sup-of-derivatives form stated in the article is not isolated.

annotations.label: Delta as distributional derivative of Heaviside

anchor.snippet: it is the distributional derivative of the Heaviside step function

annotations.note: No declaration identifies δ as the distributional derivative of the Heaviside step function.

annotations.label: Riesz representation for delta

anchor.snippet: by the Riesz representation theorem , can be represented as the Lebesgue integral

annotations.note: The link between the Dirac measure and the delta distribution is recorded, while a general Riesz-style integral-representation framing is not.

annotations.label: n-dimensional delta function

anchor.snippet: The delta function can be defined in n -dimensional Euclidean space

annotations.note: `TemperedDistribution.delta` is defined for any finite-dimensional inner product space `E`, covering ℝⁿ.

annotations.label: Dirac measure on a set

anchor.snippet: The notion of a Dirac measure makes sense on any set

annotations.note: `Measure.dirac` is defined on any measurable space `α`.

annotations.label: Delta function on a manifold

anchor.snippet: Another common generalization of the delta function is to a differentiable manifold

annotations.note: Mathlib has Dirac as a measure on any measurable space (including manifolds), but no dedicated manifold delta-distribution declaration.

annotations.label: Dirac delta on locally compact Hausdorff space

anchor.snippet: On a locally compact Hausdorff space

annotations.note: `Measure.dirac` provides the Radon measure δ_a on any measurable space, including locally compact Hausdorff spaces.

annotations.label: Convex hull dense in probability measures

anchor.snippet: the convex hull of the image of X under this embedding is dense in the space of probability measures

annotations.note: `diracProba` is embedded into `ProbabilityMeasure X`, but no density-of-convex-hull result was located.

annotations.label: Scaling property

anchor.snippet: The delta function satisfies the following scaling property for a non-zero scalar

annotations.note: No lemma establishes the scaling identity δ(αx) = δ(x)/|α| for the delta distribution.

annotations.label: Even symmetry of delta

anchor.snippet: the delta function is an even distribution (symmetry)

annotations.note: No lemma asserts the evenness/reflection invariance of δ as a distribution.

annotations.label: Distributional product with x

anchor.snippet: The distributional product of δ with x is equal to zero

annotations.note: No lemma showing x·δ = 0 in the distributional sense was found.

annotations.label: Converse algebraic identity

anchor.snippet: Conversely, if xf ( x ) = xg ( x ) , where f and g are distributions

annotations.note: The structural converse `xf = xg ⇒ f - g = cδ` for distributions is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sifting property

anchor.snippet: This is sometimes referred to as the sifting property

annotations.note: `Distribution.delta_apply` and `TemperedDistribution.delta_apply` give the sifting identity δ_x(f) = f(x).

annotations.label: Convolution time-delay

anchor.snippet: the effect of convolving a function f ( t ) with the time-delayed Dirac delta is to time-delay

annotations.note: No lemma states that convolution with δ_T translates a function by T.

annotations.label: Composition with smooth function

anchor.snippet: the delta distribution may be composed with a smooth function g ( x ) in such a way that the familiar change of variables formula holds

annotations.note: Composition of the delta distribution with a smooth function is not formalized.

annotations.label: Composition delta(g(x))

anchor.snippet: It is natural therefore to define the composition

annotations.note: No definition of δ∘g for a smooth g with simple zeros was found.

annotations.label: Generalized scaling integral form

anchor.snippet: In the integral form, the generalized scaling property may be written as

annotations.note: The integral-form generalized scaling formula for δ∘g is not formalized.

annotations.label: Indefinite integral of delta

anchor.snippet: For a constant [MATH] and a "well-behaved" arbitrary real-valued function

annotations.note: Indefinite-integral identity for δ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Scaling in n dimensions

anchor.snippet: The delta distribution in an n -dimensional space satisfies the following scaling property

annotations.note: No formalized n-dimensional scaling property δ(αx)=|α|^{-n} δ(x).

annotations.label: Invariance under reflection/rotation

anchor.snippet: Under any reflection or rotation ρ , the delta function is invariant

annotations.note: No lemma showing rotational/reflection invariance of δ in ℝⁿ.

annotations.label: Composition with bi-Lipschitz function

anchor.snippet: it is possible to define the composition of δ with a bi-Lipschitz function

annotations.note: Composition of δ with a bi-Lipschitz function is not formalized.

annotations.label: Coarea/submersion composition

anchor.snippet: Using the coarea formula from geometric measure theory

annotations.note: Composition of δ with a submersion via the coarea formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simple layer distribution on hypersurface

anchor.snippet: if S is a smooth hypersurface of R n , then we can associate to S the distribution

annotations.note: The simple-layer surface distribution δ_S is not defined.

annotations.label: Delta as normal derivative of indicator

anchor.snippet: then δ S is equal to the normal derivative of the indicator function

annotations.note: No lemma identifies δ_S with the normal derivative of an indicator function.

annotations.label: Delta in spherical coordinates

anchor.snippet: In three dimensions, the delta function at [MATH] is represented in spherical coordinates

annotations.note: No expression for δ in spherical coordinates in ℝ³ is formalized.

annotations.label: Derivative of the delta distribution

anchor.snippet: The derivative of the Dirac delta distribution, denoted δ′ and also called the Dirac delta prime

annotations.note: Mathlib has `derivCLM` for tempered distributions, so `derivCLM (delta x)` is δ′, but no dedicated `delta'` decl with its API exists.

annotations.label: k-th derivative of delta

anchor.snippet: the k -th derivative of δ is defined similarly as the distribution

annotations.note: Iterating `derivCLM` on `delta x` gives δ^{(k)}, but no specific lemma about δ's k-th derivative is provided.

annotations.label: Delta is infinitely differentiable

anchor.snippet: δ is an infinitely differentiable distribution

annotations.note: All tempered distributions admit `derivCLM` of every order, so δ is implicitly infinitely differentiable, but no explicit statement is given.

annotations.label: Derivative as limit of difference quotients

anchor.snippet: The first derivative of the delta function is the distributional limit of the difference quotients

annotations.note: No lemma identifies δ′ as the distributional limit of (δ - τ_h δ)/h.

annotations.label: Basic properties of delta derivative

anchor.snippet: The derivative of the delta function satisfies a number of basic properties

annotations.note: Specific algebraic identities involving δ′ are not formalized.

annotations.label: Convolution with delta prime

anchor.snippet: the convolution of δ′ with a compactly-supported, smooth function f is

annotations.note: No lemma stating δ′ ∗ f = f′.

annotations.label: Delta distribution on open set in higher dimensions

anchor.snippet: the Dirac delta distribution centered at a point a ∈ U is defined

annotations.note: `Distribution.delta x : 𝓓'^{n}(Ω, ℝ)` is defined on any open set Ω ⊆ E in a finite-dimensional space.

annotations.label: Alpha derivative of delta_a

anchor.snippet: denotes the associated mixed partial derivative operator, then the α -th derivative

annotations.note: Multi-index partial derivatives ∂^α δ_a are not defined as such.

annotations.label: Structure of distributions with point support

anchor.snippet: Higher derivatives enter into mathematics naturally as the building blocks for the complete structure of distributions with point support

annotations.note: No structure theorem for distributions with point support is formalized.

annotations.label: Delta as weak limit

anchor.snippet: The delta function can be viewed as the limit of a sequence of functions

annotations.note: No general weak-limit characterization of δ via approximating sequences is stated.

annotations.label: Approximate delta function / approximation to the identity

anchor.snippet: An approximate delta function η ε can be constructed in the following manner

annotations.note: `ContDiffBump.normed` provides normalized bumps used as approximations to the identity, but `η_ε` as a general rescaling construction is not isolated.

annotations.label: Weak convergence of eta_epsilon

anchor.snippet: Then a simple change of variables shows that η ε also has integral 1

annotations.note: Convolution-convergence of bump functions to evaluation is formalized for `ContDiffBump`, giving the weak-delta limit for continuous test functions.

annotations.label: Mollifier

anchor.snippet: If the initial η = η 1 is itself smooth and compactly supported then the sequence is called a mollifier

annotations.note: `ContDiffBump` provides smooth, compactly supported bumps that are mollifiers; the term `Mollifier` itself is not introduced as a separate definition.

annotations.label: Standard mollifier from bump function

anchor.snippet: The standard mollifier is obtained by choosing η to be a suitably normalized bump function

annotations.note: `ContDiffBump.normed` realizes the standard smooth mollifier obtained by normalizing a bump.

annotations.label: Piecewise linear approximation via hat function

anchor.snippet: This can be obtained by taking η 1 to be a hat function

annotations.note: No dedicated piecewise-linear hat-function approximation to δ is formalized.

annotations.label: Rectangular function approximation

anchor.snippet: if η 1 is the uniform distribution

annotations.note: No rectangular-function approximation of δ is recorded.

annotations.label: Wigner semicircle approximation

anchor.snippet: Another example is with the Wigner semicircle distribution

annotations.note: The Wigner semicircle distribution is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convolution semigroup

anchor.snippet: Approximations to the delta functions often arise as convolution semigroups

annotations.note: No `ConvolutionSemigroup` structure is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Heat kernel

anchor.snippet: The heat kernel , defined by

annotations.note: No `heatKernel` declaration was found.

annotations.label: Heat kernel in higher dimensions

anchor.snippet: In higher-dimensional Euclidean space R n , the heat kernel is

annotations.note: The n-dimensional heat kernel is not formalized.

annotations.label: Poisson kernel

anchor.snippet: The Poisson kernel

annotations.note: `poissonKernel` is defined and used in the Poisson integral formula for harmonic functions.

annotations.label: Airy function approximation

anchor.snippet: is the rescaled Airy function

annotations.note: The Airy function is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wave equation Cauchy problem

anchor.snippet: Another example is the Cauchy problem for the wave equation

annotations.note: The wave-equation Cauchy problem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sinc function approximation

anchor.snippet: Other approximations to the identity of this kind include the sinc function

annotations.note: `Real.sinc` is defined, but its role as an approximate identity converging to δ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bessel function approximation

anchor.snippet: and the Bessel function

annotations.note: Bessel functions are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Plane wave decomposition of delta

anchor.snippet: Then δ is obtained by applying a power of the Laplacian to the integral with respect to the unit sphere measure

annotations.note: Plane-wave decomposition of δ via Laplacian and sphere measure is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fourier transform of delta

anchor.snippet: The delta function is a tempered distribution , and therefore it has a well-defined Fourier transform

annotations.note: `fourier_delta_zero` shows 𝓕(δ_0) equals the volume tempered distribution (the constant 1).

annotations.label: Delta as convolution identity

anchor.snippet: the convolution of the delta function with any other tempered distribution S is simply S

annotations.note: No statement that δ ∗ S = S for tempered distributions was found.

annotations.label: Inverse Fourier transform of 1

anchor.snippet: The inverse Fourier transform of the tempered distribution f ( ξ ) = 1 is the delta function

annotations.note: `fourier_delta_zero` gives 𝓕(δ) = 1; the symmetric inverse 𝓕⁻¹(1) = δ statement is not a separate lemma.

annotations.label: Laplace transform of delta

anchor.snippet: the Laplace transform of the delta function is found to be

annotations.note: No declaration computes the Laplace transform of δ.

annotations.label: Dirichlet kernel

anchor.snippet: is defined by convolution (on the interval [−π,π] ) with the Dirichlet kernel

annotations.note: No `DirichletKernel` is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dirichlet kernel tends to delta

anchor.snippet: A fundamental result of elementary Fourier series states that the Dirichlet kernel restricted to the interval

annotations.note: No theorem stating Dirichlet kernels tend to δ.

annotations.label: Fejér kernel

anchor.snippet: The method of Cesàro summation leads to the Fejér kernel

annotations.note: No `FejerKernel` is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fejér kernel convergence

anchor.snippet: The Fejér kernels tend to the delta function in a stronger sense

annotations.note: No theorem about Fejér-kernel convergence to δ is formalized.

annotations.label: Delta as unbounded functional on L^2

anchor.snippet: The Dirac delta distribution is a densely defined unbounded linear functional on the Hilbert space

annotations.note: δ as a densely defined unbounded functional on L² is not formalized.

annotations.label: Delta as bounded functional on Sobolev space

anchor.snippet: The Sobolev embedding theorem for Sobolev spaces on the real line

annotations.note: δ as a bounded functional on a Sobolev space H^s is not formalized.

annotations.label: Delta via Cauchy integral formula

anchor.snippet: the delta function enters via Cauchy's integral formula

annotations.note: Cauchy's integral formula exists in Mathlib but no rephrasing as a delta-distribution identity was found.

annotations.label: Bergman kernel as delta

anchor.snippet: is represented by integration against a kernel [MATH] , the Bergman kernel

annotations.note: The Bergman kernel is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Resolution of the identity

anchor.snippet: is called a resolution of the identity

annotations.note: No `ResolutionOfIdentity` declaration was found.

annotations.label: Cauchy's infinitesimal delta

anchor.snippet: Cauchy used an infinitesimal α to write down a unit impulse

annotations.note: Cauchy's historical infinitesimal delta is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nonstandard analysis delta

anchor.snippet: Non-standard analysis allows one to rigorously treat infinitesimals

annotations.note: Mathlib has no nonstandard-analysis treatment of the delta function.

annotations.label: Dirac comb

anchor.snippet: A so-called uniform "pulse train" of Dirac delta measures, which is known as a Dirac comb

annotations.note: No `DiracComb` declaration was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Poisson summation formula

anchor.snippet: is precisely the Poisson summation formula

annotations.note: `Real.tsum_eq_tsum_fourier` and variants formalize the Poisson summation formula.

annotations.label: Sokhotski–Plemelj theorem

anchor.snippet: The Sokhotski–Plemelj theorem , important in quantum mechanics, relates the delta function

annotations.note: The Sokhotski–Plemelj theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kronecker delta

anchor.snippet: The Kronecker delta δ ij is the quantity defined by

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `Pi.single` / `Matrix.diagonal` for δ_ij rather than introducing a dedicated Kronecker delta notation.

annotations.label: Kronecker delta sifting property

anchor.snippet: This function then satisfies the following analog of the sifting property

annotations.note: Sifting `∑ a_i δ_ij = a_j` is realized via `Finset.sum_pi_single`-style identities for `Pi.single`.

annotations.label: Discrete distribution density

anchor.snippet: the probability density function f ( x ) of a discrete distribution consisting of points

annotations.note: Discrete probability measures as weighted sums of Dirac measures are formalized via `sum_smul_dirac`.

annotations.label: Mixture distribution density

anchor.snippet: As another example, consider a distribution in which 6/10 of the time returns a standard normal distribution

annotations.note: Mixture-of-Gaussian-and-Dirac density representation is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Density of transformed random variable

anchor.snippet: The delta function is also used to represent the resulting probability density function of a random variable that is transformed

annotations.note: No δ-based formula for the density of T(X) is formalized.

annotations.label: Local time of stochastic process

anchor.snippet: The local time of a stochastic process B ( t ) is given by

annotations.note: Local time of a stochastic process is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthonormal wave functions

anchor.snippet: A set { | φ n ⟩ } of wave functions is orthonormal if

annotations.note: `Orthonormal 𝕜 v` defines orthonormal families in an inner-product space, covering quantum-mechanical wave-function bases.

annotations.label: Generalized eigenfunctions of position operator

anchor.snippet: the position operator has a complete set of generalized eigenfunctions

annotations.note: Rigged Hilbert space / generalized eigenfunctions for the position operator are not formalized.

annotations.label: Mass-spring impulse equation

anchor.snippet: The governing equation of a simple mass–spring system excited by a sudden force impulse

annotations.note: Mass-spring impulse ODE with δ forcing is not formalized.

annotations.label: Euler-Bernoulli beam deflection

anchor.snippet: the equation governing the static deflection of a slender beam

annotations.note: Euler–Bernoulli beam deflection equation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Point moment via delta derivative

anchor.snippet: a point moment acting on a beam can be described by delta functions

annotations.note: Point-moment loading via δ′ on a beam is not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemann–Stieltjes integral against delta

anchor.snippet: the integration of the delta function against a continuous function can be properly understood as a Riemann–Stieltjes integral

annotations.note: No lemma rephrases integration against δ as a Riemann–Stieltjes integral with respect to the Heaviside step function.

annotations.label: Delta as distribution of order zero with compact support

anchor.snippet: Thus, [MATH] is a distribution of order zero. It is, furthermore, a distribution with compact support

annotations.note: No declaration explicitly asserts that δ is of order zero or characterizes its compact support {0}.

annotations.label: Higher powers identity x^n δ = 0

anchor.snippet: More generally, [MATH] for all positive integers

annotations.note: The generalization x^n · δ = 0 for positive integers n is not formalized.

annotations.label: Delta(x^2 − α^2) decomposition

anchor.snippet: Thus, for example

annotations.note: The standard example δ(x² − α²) = (δ(x−α) + δ(x+α))/(2|α|) is not formalized.
